subject: ap-calculus-bc
title: Graph Analysis & Accumulation Function
stimulus: The continuous function $f$ is defined on the closed interval $-6 \leq x \leq 5$. The figure above shows a portion of the graph of $f$, consisting of two line segments and a quarter of a circle centered at the point $(5, 3)$. It is known that the point $\left(3, 3 + \sqrt{5}\right)$ is on the graph of $f$.

parts.prompt: If $\int_{-6}^{5} f(x)\,dx = 7$, find the value of $\int_{-6}^{-2} f(x)\,dx$. Show the work that leads to your answer.

scoring_points.description: Correct computation of $\int_{-2}^{3} f(x)\,dx$ using the two line segments visible on the graph (a triangle and a trapezoid, or equivalent geometric areas)

required_elements: Geometric computation of $\int_{-2}^{3} f(x)\,dx$ from the graph

scoring_points.description: Correct computation of $\int_{3}^{5} f(x)\,dx$ using the quarter-circle area: $\frac{1}{4}\pi(2)^2 = \pi$ (quarter circle of radius 2, but the quarter circle is below the value at the center, so the signed area must be computed correctly)

required_elements: Geometric computation of $\int_{3}^{5} f(x)\,dx$ from the quarter-circle portion

scoring_points.description: Correct final answer using $\int_{-6}^{-2} f(x)\,dx = \int_{-6}^{5} f(x)\,dx - \int_{-2}^{3} f(x)\,dx - \int_{3}^{5} f(x)\,dx = 7 - \int_{-2}^{3} f(x)\,dx - \int_{3}^{5} f(x)\,dx$

required_elements: Correct numerical value using additivity of definite integrals

scoring_points.description: Correct use of FTC and linearity: $\int_{-2}^{3} \left(2f'(x) + 4\right)dx = 2[f(3) - f(-2)] + 4(3-(-2)) = 2[f(3) - f(-2)] + 20$
Correct final numerical answer (reading $f(-2)$ and $f(3)$ from the graph correctly and computing $2[f(3) - f(-2)] + 20$)

required_elements: Correct numerical value with $f(3)$ and $f(-2)$ substituted from the graph

parts.prompt: The function $g$ is given by $g(x) = \int_{-2}^{x} f(t)\,dt$. Find the absolute maximum value of $g$ on the interval $-2 \leq x \leq 5$. Justify your answer.

scoring_points.description: Correct identification that $g'(x) = f(x)$, and finding where $f(x) = 0$ on $[-2, 5]$ to identify critical points of $g$
Correct evaluation of $g$ at all critical points and endpoints ($x = -2, x = 5$, and any interior zeros of $f$), computing the accumulated area in each case

required_elements: Values of $g$ computed at critical points and endpoints via accumulated area

scoring_points.description: Correct identification of the absolute maximum value of $g$ on $[-2, 5]$ with justification comparing all candidate values

required_elements: Absolute maximum value stated with comparison of all candidates

scoring_points.description: Correct answer using the chain rule / L'Hopital interpretation: the limit equals $\frac{f'(1)}{1/(1+1^2)} = f'(1) \cdot 2 = 2f'(1)$, where $f'(1)$ is the slope of $f$ at $x = 1$ read from the graph
